pr.title: match and link itwstar.c and itnessyoyo.c
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: PR #2653 matched and linked the item translation units itwstar.c and itnessyoyo.c, adjusted item symbol metadata and logic-table references, and included several nearby item/itcoll matching cleanups. The bot report recorded +8152 matched-code bytes, +9056 linked-code bytes, 17 new matches, 8 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 unmatched-item regression.

key_files.role: Changed itwstar.c and itnessyoyo.c from NonMatching to Matching objects.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: configure.py Object(NonMatching)->Object(Matching) for melee/it/items/itwstar.c and melee/it/items/itnessyoyo.c

key_files.role: Updated item symbol metadata, including data type/scope changes and splitting a large Sheik needle thrown data range into smaller symbols.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt changes for it_803B86A4, it_803F6210, it_803F6F50..it_803F7080, it_804D52D0, it_804DCB40..it_804DCB4C, it_804DD150..it_804DD180

key_files.role: Adjusted ItemLogicTable references for Sheik needle thrown from it_803F6F50.states to it_803F6F50.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: it_279C.c two replacements of it_803F6F50.states with it_803F6F50

key_files.role: Cleaned expression shapes and variable lifetimes in item collision functions; matched it_80271830, it_8027163C, and it_80270CD8 per bot report.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itcoll.c cleanup in it_80270CD8, it_8027163C, it_80271830
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports it_80271830, it_8027163C, and it_80270CD8 reached 100.00%

key_files.role: Introduced itFlipper_GetAttrs helper and scheduling-sensitive shape changes around it_80290CE8 and related callbacks.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itflipper.c adds itFlipper_GetAttrs and todo no-op branch perturbing scheduling
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports it_80290CE8 reached 100.00%; DmgDealt and DmgReceived improved

what_changed: Linked itwstar.c and itnessyoyo.c by switching their configure.py entries from NonMatching to Matching.

Adjusted GALE01 symbol metadata for item data, rodata, sdata, and sdata2 symbols, including converting several address-style globals to local @ labels and splitting it_803F6F50 into smaller data/float symbols.
Changed Sheik needle thrown logic-table references in it_279C.c from a .states member access to direct it_803F6F50 references.
Cleaned itcoll.c code shapes, including direct expression construction in it_80270CD8, scoped temporaries for xB6C_vars in it_8027163C, and ternary-style min/max-like bounds updates in it_80271830.
Added an itFlipper_GetAttrs inline helper and made scheduling-sensitive changes in itflipper.c around it_80290CE8; the diff includes an explicit TODO noting a no-op branch perturbs scheduling.
Touched multiple related item source/header files while improving or matching individual functions and data sections reported by the decomp-dev bot.

smart_moves.text: The PR used configure.py linkage only after the bot reported full data/function matches for the target TUs, tying build configuration to matching evidence rather than just source edits.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: configure.py switches itwstar.c and itnessyoyo.c to Matching
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot reports itwstar and itnessyoyo data/function items at 100.00%

smart_moves.text: Symbol metadata was adjusted alongside source references, especially around it_803F6F50, instead of compensating only in C code.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt splits it_803F6F50 and changes it_279C.c direct references

smart_moves.text: The itflipper.c no-op branch is explicitly annotated as a scheduling perturbation TODO, making a fragile matching tactic visible to reviewers.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itflipper.c comment 'Eliminate this no-op branch (perturbs scheduling)'

decomp_lessons.lesson: When matching item TUs, symbol metadata and C references may need to move together; this PR changes it_803F6F50 symbol sizing/splitting and updates ItemLogicTable references from a struct member expression to a direct symbol.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt it_803F6F50 split
diff_excerpt: it_279C.c it_803F6F50.states -> it_803F6F50

decomp_lessons.lesson: For local matching, expression shape can matter: it_80270CD8 moved from named intermediate locals to a repeated inline arithmetic expression and reached 100% in the bot report.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itcoll.c it_80270CD8 expression rewrite
text_corpus: bot report it_80270CD8 99.88% -> 100.00%

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small lifetime/scope changes to temporaries can close near-matches; it_8027163C introduces a scoped vars pointer and local bone_dyn_desc inside the loop, and the bot reports the function reached 100%.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itcoll.c it_8027163C loop temporary changes
text_corpus: bot report it_8027163C 98.64% -> 100.00%

naming_conventions.text: This PR keeps many uncertain item symbols address-style, while changing some data ownership labels to local @NNN symbols where the symbol metadata indicates local scope.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt changes such as it_803F6210 -> @235 and it_804DD150 -> @232

naming_conventions.text: A short semantic local parameter rename from arg_angle to angle in it_80271830 is supported by immediate function usage, while global address-style names are not broadly renamed.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itcoll.c function signature it_80271830(Item* item, f32 arg_angle) -> it_80271830(Item* item, f32 angle)

naming_conventions.text: The added itFlipper_GetAttrs helper uses subsystem/item-prefixed naming and returns the specialAttributes field from an Item.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itflipper.c adds static inline itFlipper_GetAttrs(Item* ip)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Switch target TUs from NonMatching to Matching only after matching/linking progress is present.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: configure.py Matching entries for itwstar.c and itnessyoyo.c
text_corpus: bot report +8152 matched-code bytes and +9056 linked-code bytes

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use symbol metadata edits for section/type/scope correctness, including local @ labels for strings/floats and smaller split objects for a formerly larger data symbol.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt data:string/data:float and scope changes

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Try ternary/select expressions to replace verbose branch ladders or self-assignment noise when matching bounds-style calculations.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itcoll.c it_80271830 replaces multi-branch assignments with chained ternaries

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Narrow PAD_STACK and local layout changes were used in touched functions; one itflipper no-op branch is documented as scheduling-sensitive and should remain treated as a fragile matching tactic, not general style.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itflipper.c PAD_STACK(8)->PAD_STACK(4) and scheduling TODO
diff_excerpt: itcoll.c PAD_STACK in it_8027163C remains while local declarations change

Data/symbol metadata changes are visible, but the PR slice does not include independent proof of why each individual scope/type change is correct.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR2653: Matching item TUs may require coordinated symbols.txt and source-reference edits
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2653, itwstar.c and itnessyoyo.c were linked as Matching while symbols.txt metadata was updated and it_279C.c references to it_803F6F50 changed from .states member access to direct symbol use. Treat this as PR-specific evidence that item TU matching can depend on coordinated symbol metadata and source expression updates.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: configure.py Matching entries
diff_excerpt: symbols.txt it_803F6F50 split
diff_excerpt: it_279C.c it_803F6F50.states -> it_803F6F50
text_corpus: bot report linked-code and matched-code gains

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR2653: Scheduling-sensitive no-op branch documented in itflipper
accepted_candidate_records.text: The itflipper.c diff includes a no-op if (pos != NULL) block with a TODO stating it perturbs scheduling. This is evidence of a narrow matching tactic in this PR, not a recommended general style.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itflipper.c scheduling TODO
text_corpus: bot report it_80290CE8 reached 100.00%

source_update_candidates.title: PR2653 linked itwstar.c and itnessyoyo.c as Matching item objects
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2653 changed configure.py entries for melee/it/items/itwstar.c and melee/it/items/itnessyoyo.c from NonMatching to Matching.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: configure.py Object(NonMatching)->Object(Matching) for both files
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential path fact about matching status should be curated from current source/history before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: PR2653 adjusted it_803F6F50 item data symbol shape
source_update_candidates.text: The PR changed symbols.txt metadata around it_803F6F50 from a single 0x130 global object plus following global string into a smaller 0x50 object, multiple aligned float objects, and a local string label; it_279C.c references were updated accordingly.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: symbols.txt it_803F6F50..it_803F7080 changes
diff_excerpt: it_279C.c direct it_803F6F50 references
source_update_candidates.reason: Symbol ownership/splitting facts belong in source/path metadata, not as a global lesson without current-source verification.

source_update_candidates.title: Document scheduling-sensitive no-op branches as temporary matching tactics
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2653 contains an explicitly documented no-op branch in itflipper.c used because it perturbs scheduling. Curator may consider whether existing matching-tactics guidance should mention documenting and isolating no-op scheduling perturbations.
evidence_refs: diff_excerpt: itflipper.c TODO 'Eliminate this no-op branch (perturbs scheduling)'
source_update_candidates.reason: The evidence supports this as a PR-specific tactic; promotion to a global standard requires curator review and more examples.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all address-style item data symbols should be converted to local @ labels; this PR shows specific metadata edits only.
Do not treat the itflipper no-op branch as accepted style; the diff itself marks it as a TODO to eliminate.
Do not claim all changed functions fully matched; bot report lists specific matches, improvements, and one regression, and the full diff is truncated.
Do not infer Cuyler's exact contributions beyond the PR body statement that Cuyler matched a good chunk of these.
